description: This figure displays a 3D cubic supercell crystal structure model of Fe-Mn-Al alloys with Pm-3m space group (B2-type intermetallic structure). The purple, green, and blue spheres correspond to Mn, Al, and Fe atoms respectively, where Mn atoms substitute Fe positions in the primitive FeAl lattice to construct Fe₁₋ₓMnₓAl alloys with variable Mn contents (x ranges from 0 to 1). This model acts as the foundational computational geometry for the density functional theory based first-principles calculations of the alloy system, supporting subsequent simulations of thermodynamic stability, electronic structure, mechanical properties, elastic anisotropy, and Debye temperature of the Fe-Mn-Al series. It serves as the structural basis for the core conclusion that all Fe₁₋ₓMnₓAl alloys are thermodynamically stable, with FeAl exhibiting the lowest formation enthalpy and highest structural stability in the system.
